title: Weekly Markets Monitor - Sticky wickets
gist: WGC's weekly macro recap: US headline CPI broke above 4% for the first time since 2023, the ECB hiked for the first time in three years, and a draft US-Iran agreement triggered a sharp drop in crude, breakevens and Fed-hike pricing. WGC's framing: the lagged effects of a three-month oil disruption mean inflation risks stay elevated and the path to policy easing may be longer than markets price.

insights.claim: US CPI rose above 4% in the latest print, the first time headline inflation has breached 4% since 2023.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A 4%-handle on CPI undercuts the disinflation narrative and the case for near-term Fed cuts, a tailwind for gold as an inflation hedge.
insights.quote: Last week, US CPI rose above 4% for the first time since 2023.
insights.locator: Highlights — 'Last week, US CPI rose above 4%'

insights.claim: WGC argues the lagged effects of a three-month oil disruption keep inflation risks elevated, so the path to policy easing may be longer than markets currently anticipate.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: If easing is delayed versus market pricing, front-end rate expectations and rate-sensitive assets are mispriced; gold benefits if real rates stay capped.
insights.quote: inflation risks remain elevated and that the path to policy easing may be longer than markets currently anticipate

insights.claim: A draft US-Iran agreement prompted a sharp fall in crude oil prices, breakeven inflation rates and Fed hike expectations during the week.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A geopolitical de-escalation that lowers oil also drags breakevens and rate-hike odds, reshaping the macro backdrop across energy, rates and FX in one move.
insights.quote: the announcement of a draft US-Iran agreement prompted a sharp fall in crude oil prices, breakeven inflation rates and Fed hike expectations
insights.locator: Highlights — 'But the announcement of a draft US-Iran agreement'

insights.claim: WGC cautions that normalization of global oil markets may not be immediate despite the encouraging US-Iran draft, given the three-month disruption.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A slower-than-expected oil normalization keeps an inflation kicker alive, arguing against fully pricing the de-escalation into rates.
insights.quote: a normalization of global oil markets may not be immediate

insights.claim: The ECB raised interest rates for the first time in three years during the week.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: An ECB hike after three years signals a hawkish turn in Europe, widening the policy divergence with markets that expect easing.
insights.quote: the ECB raised interest rates for the first time in three years
insights.locator: Highlights — 'China's exports in May exceeded expectations'

insights.claim: China's exports in May exceeded expectations while growth in Japan and the UK slowed.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: Divergent growth (China beat, Japan/UK slowdown) supports a rotation view and complicates a synchronized global easing thesis.
insights.quote: China's exports in May exceeded expectations, growth in Japan and the UK slowed
insights.locator: Highlights — 'On the macro front, headline inflation accelerated'

insights.claim: Over the week major global equity markets rose and benchmark treasury yields declined, while the US dollar and oil prices eased.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: Lower yields plus a softer dollar is a classic supportive setup for gold; the combination signals easing financial conditions despite hot CPI.
insights.quote: Major global equity market rose, benchmark treasury yields declined, while the US dollar and oil prices eased.

insights.claim: Market sentiment for the week was shaped by optimism over a potential US-Iran agreement and a historic mega IPO.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: Risk appetite was driven by event-specific catalysts rather than fundamentals, suggesting fragility if either narrative reverses.
insights.quote: The past week was marked by optimism over a potential US-Iran agreement and a historic mega IPO.
insights.locator: Highlights — 'The past week was marked by optimism'
